steps.description: Type text 'copilot connection app' in the search bar located at the top of the 'Samples' tab within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface. And input ${{var:app_name:copilot-connection-app}} into the run time variable.
Click the "Create" button next to the "Copilot connect..." sample in the "Featured samples" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of the default folder path in the "Workspace Folder" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
@assertion 'Preview README.MD' is visible
@assertion the page title contains 'Copilot connector app'
Right-click on the "Preview README.md" tab in the Visual Studio Code interface to open the context menu.
Click the "Close" option (labeled "Ctrl+W") in the context menu of the tab preview for "README.md" within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in the Visual Studio Code application, within the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" workspace interface.
Type the command ">notifications: clear all notifications" into the Visual Studio Code command palette to clear all active notifications.
Press the "Enter" key to execute the selected command "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" from the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface within Visual Studio Code.
Type text: 'Microsoft 365 Agents: Deploy' in the command palette of Visual Studio Code, targeting the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit integration interface.
Press "Enter" key to execute the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Deploy" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click the "Deploy" button in the confirmation dialog box prompting, "Do you want to deploy resources in dev environment?" in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
@assertion there's "Ok to proceed? (y)" exist in terminal log.
Click the "y" key prompt in the terminal of Visual Studio Code to confirm proceeding
Press the "Enter" key in the terminal to confirm the prompt: "Need to install the Microsoft.Azure@azure/static-web-apps-cli@2.0.0. OK to proceed? (y)".
@assertion there's notification: "actions in deploy stage executed successfully." exist
Click the scroll bar to scroll down the page
Click the "Ingest Data" button in the "2. Ingest Sample Data into Copilot connector" section to begin processing sample data using the Azure Functions app.
@assertion the sysytem returns the 401 error indicating missing admin consents for the required permissions.
